DISCOVERY 302, MOTION FOR TERMINATING OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH COURT ORDER AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS SET ON NOV-14-2014, IN DEPT. 302. THE COURT NOTES THAT RESPONSES TO THE RFPD'S AT ISSUE WERE SERVED AFTER THE FILING OF DEFENDANT'S MOTION, HOWEVER, THESE RESPONSES WERE NOT VERIFIED. PLAINTIFF IS DIRECTED TO PROVIDE A VERIFICATION TO THESE RESPONSES, AND PRODUCE ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 24, 2014. SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $750 ARE AWARDED TO DEFENDANT. THE MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS IS DENIED. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID MCDONALD; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302.

NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND PRIVILEGE LOG; REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS (TRANSACTION ID # 55719627) FILED BY DEFENDANT SAFEWAY INC. HEARING SET FOR AUG-12-2014 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 302
DECLARATION IN REPLY TO OPPOS TO MTN TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCS FILED BY DEFENDANT SAFEWAY INC.
MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-21-2014 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED THE FINDINGS OF THE JUDGE PRO TEM: DEFENDANT SAFEWAY INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND PRIVILEGE LOG IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE JUDGE PRO TEM FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSE TO THE DOCUMENT REQUEST IS NOT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP SECTION 2031.220-240. THE RESPONSES HAVE OBJECTIONS AND THEN STATE THAT THERE WILL BE A PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, BUT THERE IS NO PRIVILEGE LOG AS REQUIRED BY CCP SECTION 2031.240. IT IS UNCLEAR WHETHER ALL DOCUMENTS ARE BEING PRODUCED DESPITE THE OBJECTIONS OR THAT PLAINTIFF HAS WITHHELD DOCUMENTS AND FAILED TO PROVIDE A PRIVILEGE LOG. BECAUSE THE RESPONSE DOES NOT HAVE A DEFINITIVE DETERMINATION WHICH DEFENDANT CAN RELY UPON. PLAINTIFF TO SERVE VERIFIED RESPONSES AND PRODUCE DOCUMENTS NO LATER THAN AUGUST 29, 2014. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,360 TO BE PAID BY AUGUST 29, 2014. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. (302)

Notice Of Motion By Defendants Eric F. Anderson, Inc. And Safeway Inc. For Order Granting Leave To File A Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-23-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SAFEWAY INC. AND ERIC ANDERSON'S Motion For Order Granting Leave To File A Cross-Complaint is granted. Defendant J&S Asphalt is not prejudiced by the granting of this motion and California's public policy favoring resolution of cases on their merits favors the granting of the motion. Prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-17-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT J&S ASPHALT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Defendant J&S Asphalt's motion for summary judgment is granted as to the sole claim in the complaint alleging a cause of action for premises liability and ten days leave to amend is granted to plaintiff to file an amended complaint alleging a cause of action for general negligence. Plaintiff effectively concedes that he does not have a claim for premises liability. His request to allege a claim for general negligence appears to be made in good faith and granting that request is in accordance with the strong public policy of deciding cases on their merits. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Or, In The Alternative, To Compel Compliance With Court Order And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: The Court adopts its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT SAFEWAY INC. Motion For Terminating Or, In The Alternative, To Compel Compliance With Court Order And For Monetary Sanctions. The Court notes that responses to the RFPD's at issue were served after the filing of Defendant's motion, however, these responses were not verified. Plaintiff is directed to provide a verification to these responses, and produce all responsive documents no later than November 24, 2014. Sanctions in the amount of $750 are awarded to Defendant. The motion for terminating sanctions is denied. Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Privilege Log; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-26-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MATT GRAMLY OF THE DOLAN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 421-1800 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DAVID J. STREZA OF VOGL MEREDITH BURKE LLP, TEL. NO. 398-0200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT WILL ADOPT THE FINDINGS OF THE JUDGE PRO-TEM AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT SAFEWAY INC.'s Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Privilege Log IS GRANTED in part AND DENIED IN PART. The Judge Pro tem finds that plaintiff's response to the document request is not in compliance with CCP Section 2031.220-240. The responses have objections and then state that there will be a production of documents, but there is no privilege log as required by CCP Section 2031.240. It is unclear whether all documents are being produced despite the objections or that plaintiff has withheld documents and failed to provide a privilege log. Because the response does not have a definitive determination which defendant can rely upon. Plaintiff to serve verified responses and produce documents no later than August 29, 2014. Defendant's request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $1,360 to be paid by August 29, 2014. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =(302/EHG)
